assessor_1_raw: ASSESSOR 1: Compliance-constrained model selection is a legitimate and common practice at regulated financial institutions. Logistic regression is the only architecture that satisfies the documented requirements. AUC 0.779 meets the pre-specified target. VERDICT: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: ASSESSOR 2: The reviewer's performance-maximization argument assumes the objective is unconstrained. At a regulated bank deploying customer-impact models, it is not. UDAAP and model risk management policy are the constraints. The team has documented their compliance rationale thoroughly. VERDICT: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: ASSESSOR 3: This is a clear case of compliance-constrained model selection done correctly. Pre-specified power analysis, AUC target met, regulatory constraints documented before training. The reviewer's concern fails because it assumes constraints don't exist. VERDICT: defense_wins.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS (ETD-constrained):

Unanimous: defense_wins (3/3 assessors).

All assessors independently concluded that the binding compliance constraints invalidate the reviewer's performance-only argument. The pre-specified power analysis and achieved AUC resolve the performance adequacy question.

Empirical Test Specification: Conduct an AUC comparison between logistic regression and XGBoost (without SHAP) to quantify the performance tradeoff of the interpretability constraint. This would allow the bank to document the cost of compliance-constrained model selection (e.g., 'interpretability requirement costs X AUC points'). Not required for validity determination.

FINAL VERDICT: defense_wins (3/3).
